name: Espiritismo
description: Espiritismo is a spiritist belief and practice rooted in the Caribbean, particularly in Cuba, Puerto Rico, and the Dominican Republic. It blends elements of Kardecist Spiritism, African traditional religions, and indigenous beliefs. Central to Espiritismo is the belief in the existence of spirits, both benevolent and malevolent, who can influence the lives of the living. Mediums play a crucial role, acting as intermediaries to communicate with spirits, offer guidance, and perform healings. Sessions, often conducted in private homes or spiritist centers, involve prayer, singing, and the use of symbolic objects. Espiritismo provides a framework for understanding illness, misfortune, and social problems, attributing them to spiritual causes. It emphasizes moral responsibility, self-improvement, and the importance of maintaining harmonious relationships with both the living and the deceased. The practice offers comfort, healing, and a sense of community to its followers, serving as a vital part of Caribbean cultural identity and spiritual life. Espiritismo's focus on ancestral veneration and spirit communication provides a framework for navigating life's challenges and maintaining connections to the past. It is a dynamic and evolving tradition, adapting to changing social and cultural contexts while retaining its core principles of spiritual connection and healing.
subtitle: Communication and Healing through Spirit Contact

keyMyths.title: The Origin of Spirits
keyMyths.description: Spirits are believed to be disembodied souls who continue to evolve in the spiritual realm. They can be contacted for guidance, healing, and protection.

sources.author: Alan Kardec
sources.description: Foundational text for Kardecist Spiritism, influencing Espiritismo.
